methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: Vault TVL must never be negative; if total debt exceeds total assets, TVL must report 0.
invariants.attack_scenario: If TVL calculation allows negative values, it could cause an underflow in the vault's share price calculation, allowing an attacker to mint shares for free or manipulate the exchange rate to drain the vault.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_tvl_non_negative() public view { assert(TVLHelper.getTVL(vaultId, registry, baseToken) >= 0); }

invariants.description: The health factor of the connector's position in lending protocols must never fall below the configured minimumHealthFactor after any borrowing or collateral withdrawal.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a manager or strategy executes a transaction that drops the health factor below the minimum, the vault's position becomes vulnerable to liquidation by third-party bots, resulting in a direct loss of user funds.

invariants.description: The flash loan contract must not hold any residual balance of the borrowed tokens after the flash loan execution completes.
invariants.attack_scenario: If tokens are left in the flash loan contract due to incorrect accounting or malicious strategy data, they are permanently trapped or can be swept by an attacker, leading to a loss of vault assets.

invariants.description: The receiving amount from any cross-chain swap must be strictly greater than or equal to the requested minimum amount (slippage protection).
invariants.attack_scenario: If slippage bounds are not strictly enforced, MEV bots can sandwich the vault's rebalancing swaps, extracting significant value and causing a loss of principal for depositors.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_swap_slippage_enforced() public view { assert(receivingAmount >= request.minAmount); }

invariants.description: A bridge transaction can only be executed if it was approved at least BRIDGE_TXN_WAITING_TIME seconds ago.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the timelock is bypassed, a compromised manager key could instantly bridge all vault funds to an attacker-controlled destination chain without giving Watchers the required time to veto the transaction.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_bridge_timelock_respected(bytes32 txn) public view { if (approvedBridgeTXN[txn] != 0) { assert(block.timestamp >= approvedBridgeTXN[txn] + BRIDGE_TXN_WAITING_TIME); } }

invariants.description: The multisig nonce must strictly increase by exactly 1 upon each successful transaction execution.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the nonce does not strictly increase, an attacker could replay a previously signed transaction (e.g., a token transfer or strategy execution), draining funds or manipulating the vault state.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_nonce_monotonicity() public { uint256 pre = nonce; execute(...); assert(nonce == pre + 1); }

invariants.description: The number of holding positions in a vault must never exceed maxNumHoldingPositions.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker or compromised manager could add an unbounded number of dust positions to the registry. This would cause an Out-Of-Gas (OOG) error during the TVL calculation loop, effectively freezing all deposits and withdrawals in the vault.
